programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Inspired by the stories of his shipwrecked relatives, Ross heads up to the Royal Navys historic naval base in the far northerly Orkney Islands, Scapa Flow. Here, Ross and dive supervisor Neil Brock link up with expert local dive guide Emily Turton and head out into the water to dive on Second World War wrecks. Ross and Emily explore a captured German scout ship called the F2 and recover a slew of artefacts that could be linked to Rosss two relatives who died serving in the Royal Navy during the conflict. Ross investigates how Winston Churchill hit upon a revolutionary tactic to defend Scapa Flow from U-boat attacks, as the wreck of the sunken SS Tabarka finally gives up its secrets

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: To take on the deepest wrecks in Scapa Flow, Ross needs to successfully navigate decompression training before heading out with local dive guide Emily Turton to dive on one of the biggest of the Kaiser?s Imperial war ships from the First World War, SMS Karlsruhe. As he explores the eerie relic, Ross tries to understand how and why the German fleet ended up on the Scottish seabed, when they had already surrendered months earlier. Having resolved one enduring mystery, he then heads 600 miles south to meet with ex-serviceman Mark Culwick and dive on an experimental British aircraft carrier submarine built in the 1920s called the M2. This unique vessel was lost in shocking circumstances, with all hands. During his dive Ross starts to understand the horrifying truth ofexactly how and why the 60 men on board never came home

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The actor is joined by advanced technical diver Emily Turton in north Devon, as he prepares to dive down on the wreck of a sunken steam ship. Iona II was heading to America in the early 1860s, when it went down with a mystery cargo on board that the owners went to extensive efforts to recover in secret. The pair embark on a night dive with the hope it will reveal what the owners so desperately tried to covertly retrieve. Determined to get to the bottom of the mystery, Ross also meets with a maritime historian who has uncovered Iona IIs shocking purpose. Ross and maritime archaeologist Mallory Haas head to Plymouth to dive on a newly discovered wreck, and to uncover a staggering artefact that links the site to one of the most controversial and censored periods in British history - the transatlantic slave trade

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit the Dorset Country Museum, where they explore finds from archaeological digs across the west of Britain. The presenters examine objects found at a Bronze Age burial site in Barrowclump, a Roman army site called Durotriges and the site of an early hospital at Winchester, Hampshire. Plus, a hoard found by metal detectors revealing hidden secrets of childhood 1,000 years ago, and a Roman settlement at Ipplepen in Devon

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: Despite being one of the most studied civilizations, rich in history, culture, science and art, much of Ancient Egypt remains a great mystery to this day. This series uses the latest science and cutting-edge technology to help experts solve some of Egypts most enduring mysteries that have tormented scientists for years. Here, science reveals new insights into belief and superstition in the land of the pharaohs, from finding the long-lost Land of Punt to Tutankhamun

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files

programme.desc: Everyone is on edge as the team reunite reluctant mother Dilla with her new baby Delilah. Clara and Clarita go on their final journey

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: A day in the life of a Bengal tiger mum as she fights to keep her three young cubs safe in Indias unforgiving jungle

programme.title: Just Go with It (2011)
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon pretends to be unhappily married to seduce women. When his girlfriend finds his prop wedding ring, he claims he is getting a divorce and has to persuade his assistant to pose as his soon-to-be ex-wife, but is forced to come up with yet more lies to maintain the illusion. Romantic comedy, starring Adam Sandler, Jennifer Aniston and Nicole Kidman

programme.title: It Takes Two (1996)
programme.desc: Two lookalike youngsters trade places to help resolve each others problems. Childrens comedy, starring Kirstie Alley, Steve Guttenberg and Mary-Kate and Ashley Olsen

programme.title: Stuart Little (1999)
programme.desc: An American couple decide their son needs a brother, and plan to adopt another child - but when they get to the orphanage they are greeted by a charming talking mouse named Stuart, who fits the bill perfectly. However, their jealous cat hatches a plot to get rid of the new arrival. Comedy adventure, starring Geena Davis and Hugh Laurie, with the voice of Michael J Fox

programme.title: Stuart Little 2 (2002)
programme.desc: The plucky talking mouse feels left out when the son of his adopted human family makes a new pal - but quickly forgets such trifling matters when his high-flying canary friend is captured by an evil falcon, prompting the brave rodent to mount a daring rescue. Family adventure sequel, with Geena Davis, Hugh Laurie and Jonathan Lipnicki, and the voices of Michael J Fox, Melanie Griffith and James Woods

programme.title: Mousehunt (1997)
programme.desc: Two destitute brothers stand to make a fortune by auctioning off the family home, but before the sale can be finalised they face the task of evicting its resident mouse. However, the resourceful rodent would rather stay put, and outwits their every bungling attempt to oust it. Slapstick comedy, starring Lee Evans, Nathan Lane and Christopher Walken

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: Swede Caroline (2024)
programme.desc: The competitive giant vegetable-growing world is rocked by scandal when a prized marrow plant is stolen. British mockumentary, starring Jo Hartley, Aisling Bea and Ray Fearon

programme.title: Daddy Day Care (2003)
programme.desc: Two unemployed executives decide to open a day-care centre, believing they have found an easy way to make a profit - but instead discover looking after a horde of rampant kids is not as simple as it seems. Family comedy, starring Eddie Murphy, Jeff Garlin, Steve Zahn and Anjelica Huston

programme.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop (2009)
programme.desc: A single father is determined to become a police officer but fails the medical because he is overweight. To compensate, he goes to absurd lengths to succeed in his job as a security guard, but when criminals lay siege to his place of work, he finds his opportunity to prove his worth to law enforcement. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Keir ODonnell

programme.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The bumbling security guard is invited to attend a conference in Las Vegas, and brings his teenage daughter along for a family holiday before she leaves for college. However, he is forced to leap into action when he uncovers a gang of criminals are plotting a casino heist. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Raini Rodriguez

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mothers heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly is transported back to the days of the Wild West, where he strives to save his eccentric scientist friend Doc Brown from death at the hands of a villainous gunslinger. Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop (2009)

programme.title: The Estate (2022)
programme.desc: Two sisters attempt to win over their terminally ill, difficult-to-please aunt in the hope of becoming the beneficiaries of her wealthy estate, only to find the rest of their greedy family members have the same idea. Comedy, starring Toni Collette, Anna Faris, David Duchovny and Kathleen Turner

programme.title: Blithe Spirit (2020)
programme.desc: A spiritualist medium holds a seance for a writer suffering from writers block, but accidentally summons the spirit of his deceased first wife. That leads to an increasingly complex love triangle with his current spouse of five years. Director Edward Halls take on the Noel Coward comedy, starring Isla Fisher, Dan Stevens and Leslie Mann

programme.title: Funny Cow (2018)
programme.desc: A stand-up comedian fights for success on the brutally unforgiving working mens club scene of 1970s northern England. Drama, starring Maxine Peake, Stephen Graham, Paddy Considine and Tony Pitts, who also wrote the screenplay

programme.title: Bad Boys (1995)
programme.desc: Detectives Mike Lowrey and Marcus Burnett search for a $100million heroin consignment brazenly stolen by a French drug kingpin from the police evidence room. However, after a mix-up over a phone call from a potential witness, each cop has to assume the others identity - Lowrey becoming the henpecked family man and Burnett the playboy. Michael Bays action thriller, starring Will Smith, Martin Lawrence, Tea Leoni, Tcheky Karyo and Theresa Randle

programme.title: Men in Black (1997)
programme.desc: A streetwise cop is enlisted by a top-secret government force dedicated to policing alien visitors to Earth, teaming up with a veteran agent just as a body-swapping extraterrestrial giant insect begins a reign of terror throughout New York City. Sci-fi comedy, starring Will Smith, Tommy Lee Jones, Linda Fiorentino, Rip Torn and Vincent DOnofrio

programme.title: Men in Black II (2002)
programme.desc: Agent J investigates the unauthorised landing of a spacecraft and discovers a malevolent alien disguised as a lingerie model is on the verge of destroying humanity. His only hope of stopping her diabolical plot is to restore the memory of his retired partner K - but his former mentor takes more than a little convincing. Sci-fi comedy sequel, directed by Barry Sonnenfeld, starring Will Smith, Tommy Lee Jones, Rosario Dawson, Lara Flynn Boyle, Johnny Knoxville, Rip Torn and Tony Shalhoub

programme.title: Men in Black 3 (2012)
programme.desc: An alien criminal escapes from a prison on the moon and goes back in time on a mission of revenge, changing the course of history. Agent J of the top-secret Earth defence organisation follows him back to 1969, and joins forces with a younger version of his mentor Agent K to set things right. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Will Smith, Josh Brolin and Tommy Lee Jones

programme.desc: A woman returns to her home town to live in a shed belonging to her mother, who grows frustrated as her daughter regresses to childhood. programme.desc: A wealthy womans privileged existence is shattered when she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend. Seeking solace, she turns to her estranged sister, who lives in a London council estate, and finds a new lease of life in joining her community dance class. Comedy, starring Imelda Staunton, Celia Imrie, Timothy Spall and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gangs preparations for Star Wars Day are interrupted by the news that Professor Proton has died. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco, with a guest appearance by Bob Newhart

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The World is Full of Married Men (1979)
programme.desc: Saucy, glossy romp adapted by Jackie Collins from her own novel _ and therefore a bit thin on plot, but bursting with heaving bosoms and heavy jewellery. It tells of an advertising executives decidedly sordid extramarital escapades, and his neglected wife, who eventually gets tried of his philandering _ and decides to take her long-awaited revenge by giving him a well-deserved taste of his own medicine. Starring Anthony Franciosa, Gareth Hunt, Paul Nicholas, Carroll Baker and Georgina Hale.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: Constant rain threatens the harvesting of the oat crop so Peter Ginn tries to fashion a weather vane in the hope of predicting future conditions, before the team deploys the latest in farming technology, including a tractor and an Edwardian film crew captures progress. The town enjoys a grand fete where new innovations of the era such as the electric lightbulb, the petrol-powered Lister engine and a genuine flying machine are showcased

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The presenter of TV holiday series Hot Shots takes a midnight swim, only for her body to be washed up on the shore. programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Holidaying financiers receive a note foretelling a murder - and it isnt long before of them falls to his death in a skydiving incident. The CBI is asked to investigate, and uncovers evidence suggesting a much-maligned former employee is the killer, but Patrick remains unconvinced, and sets out to prove that ambition and corporate greed drove one of the group to homicide. Jaime Murray (Dexter, Hustle) guest stars

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Gary Delaney, Tiffany Stevenson and Romesh Ranganathan for the comedy quiz. The panellists give their take on major news stories from July 2014 and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams headed by David Mitchell and Lee Mack try to hoodwink each other with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Strictly judge Bruno Tonioli, Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark and comedians Adam Buxton and Rob Beckett are this weeks guests

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Bez, Motsi Mabuse, Shazia Mirza and Steve Pemberton join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Hypothetical
programme.desc: Comedy game show hosted by Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster, in which Frank Skinner, Jon Richardson, Rosie Jones and Esther Manito are posed increasingly absurd hypothetical situations

programme.title: Hypothetical
programme.desc: Comedy game show hosted by Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster, in which leading comedians are posed increasingly absurd hypothetical situations and scored on how well they would deal with them

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Thousands of dollars of cartel cash are found hidden in an engine, an intoxicated 17-year-old requires urgent medical treatment, and cocaine is found in bags of rubbish

programme.title: Cops on the Rock
programme.sub-title: Friday Nights Are A Bad Cocktail
programme.desc: The drug squad makes a pre-dawn arrest on a worker travelling over the border from Spain on his moped, and trainee uniformed officers undergo pepper spray training

programme.title: Cops on the Rock
programme.desc: Customs officers spring into action after news of a sinking boat - on land, Sergeant Steve uses good old-fashioned leg work to track down two stolen scooters

programme.title: Railroad Australia
programme.desc: A new V/Line VLocity train is driven to its limits to pass the final test. The ageing Savannahlander railcar faces one of Australias roughest tracks after a stormy wet season

programme.title: Rick Steins Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef is invited to a family feast in Corfu and learns how to make authentic Greek food in restaurant kitchens around the island. Later, Rick heads for familiar surroundings in Majorca, where he worked as a teenage cook, and finds out what British tourists like to eat

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A shipment of meth disguised as concrete is intercepted, and officers interview a Haitian immigrant who has crossed nine countries on foot to reach America

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Millions of dollars worth of marijuana is found hidden in a shipment of mattresses, and officers detain a mother and daughter who have been smoking meth

programme.title: Guy Martin: Proper Jobs
programme.desc: The mechanic joins a multi-generational family business who rescue HGVs, learns the physics of truck recovery, and operates a crane during a complex rescue

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond find second-hand bargains for the same price as the £6,995 Nissan Pixo. James May is in Nevada to test the toughness of the Range Rover Evoque, driving it through Death Valley before chauffeuring a megastar across Las Vegas. Plus, the McLaren MP4-12C is compared with its supercar rival the Ferrari 458 Italia, and four-time Formula One world champion Sebastian Vettel is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to make train travel cheaper, faster and more interesting, replacing the locomotive with a specially modified car and the carriages with a series of caravans. Comedy star Rowan Atkinson gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car and Jeremy drives the Jaguar XKR-S and a Nissan GT-R round the track to compare both models

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey by exploring the history behind some of Britains famous pies, a dish the duo have written a book about. They also find out who ate all the pies on the football pitch

programme.title: Rick Steins Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef is invited to a family feast in Corfu and learns how to make authentic Greek food in restaurant kitchens around the island. Later, Rick heads for familiar surroundings in Majorca, where he worked as a teenage cook, and finds out what British tourists like to eat

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Simon Gregson, Michelle Visage and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. programme.desc: Melody helps a film studies lecturer and his student who are willing to sacrifice their careers to protect their illicit romance. programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruth helps a pregnant girl stand up to the father of her baby. Vivien experiences problems with public transport and takes out her frustrations at work, while Eva considers moving back in with Jimmi - after coming to a certain agreement

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Rosie receives good news, but Mubbs does not seem interested, and Diane struggles to cope as Joannas life hangs in the balance - along with that of her unborn baby. Luckily, Owen is able to save the child - but it could be at the expense of the mother. Meanwhile, the driver responsible attempts to chat up Donna, but when she learns he is a drug dealer she goes behind Zubins back to arrange his downfall

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruth stands by her decision to have Charlie moved out of the ED, resulting in a frosty reception from the staff on her first day as clinical standards manager. She finally plucks up the courage to confront Edward about his homosexuality, hoping she can turn the situation to her advantage. Meanwhile, Lenny is reluctant to visit a woman in need of his help, but a patients plight later prompts a change of heart

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Quinnans wedding day dawns, but the reappearance of Garfield is just one of the many clouds on the horizon

programme.desc: Max is determined to find Stacey, but his grim task leaves him shaken. Becca offers comfort, but does she know more than shes letting on?

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: When Max finally tracks down Stacey, old wounds are re-opened and fresh ones created

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: Tom is attacked by an intruder at the boatyard, while Gerald and Polly have a furious row and end their marriage

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.sub-title: If Music Be the Food of Love
programme.desc: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of ballroom dancing, brimming with passion and jealousy - and learn the dangers of partners falling in love with one another. Guest starring Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Leila Mimmack

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon foils a mugging at Chigwell station, and ends up befriending the intended victim - who turns out to be financial adviser to Doriens husband

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will Randolph Make a Good Impression?
programme.desc: Hobbos quest to find a man for Stella leads him to put up an advertisement, but the suitor who turns up lacks what it takes to tackle the widow and runs off. However, a chance to hit back emerges when Hobbo finds out who Stellas favourite movie character is

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: In Which Romance Springs a Leak
programme.desc: When Hobbo learns that Howard feels he is taken for granted by Pearl and that Toby misses his former wife, he hatches a plan to sort out their love lives - with the help of flowers, a Mario Lanza recording and his own team of miming violinists

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Variations on a Theme of Fathers Day
programme.desc: Convinced that Nelly is his mother, Hobbo sends Howard to eavesdrop on her and Pearl as he tries to find out more about his father. However, when the women concoct some false information, the police start to take an interest in the mens activities. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, June Whitfield and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part One
programme.desc: When a man is killed at her university reunion, Becks finds herself on the wrong side of an investigation. Was a friend responsible?

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part Two
programme.desc: As Judith and Suzie work with the police to find the killer, tensions rise among Becks friends in the house. Guest starring Harry Enfield

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra takes drastic measures to prevent Jack exacting revenge on the hit-and-run driver who killed his wife. With the teams future under threat, she tries desperately to keep the truth about the incident from shady new recruit DS Karen Hardwick, while working on a 10-year-old murder case to restore the squads reputation. Amanda Redman and Lynda Bellingham star

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When the body of a woman is found, all evidence points to suicide - but Havers is adamant foul play has occurred. Reinstated to the rank of Detective Sergeant and paired once more with Lynley, she sets out to uncover the mysteries surrounding the victim. The trail leads the pair to an exclusive club where Havers goes undercover to delve beneath the respectable surface and find the killer. Jenny Agutter guest stars, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried gives another lecture on economy to Calum, who is visited by Molly McFeely, with whose family he stayed while in Ireland - while Deirdre also pays a call at the same time. Meanwhile, James receives an invitation to examine Lord Buttermeres new stallion. Rural drama, starring John McGlynn, Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy. First broadcast in 1988

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon gets a shock when Social Security staff realise they are authorising rent payments for her old flat when she is no longer living there and her lodger is in prison

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part One
programme.desc: When a man is killed at her university reunion, Becks finds herself on the wrong side of an investigation. Was a friend responsible?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon tries to climb the social ladder - but ends up meeting a snake. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A figure from the past has come back to haunt Rawton with sinister packages and mystery calls. Lennox steps in to offer his support, but has his work cut out squaring up to the dangerous psychopath

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Nick helps a boy claiming to have an allergy to chocolate. Exasperated by public transport, Vivien declares she is going to take up driving, and is confident she will only need a few refresher lessons before sitting the test. Julia is distressed by negative publicity about the practice

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A woman discovers she is pregnant by her younger lover and the blood test reveals she is HIV positive, leading to an angry confrontation with her ex-husband, which George has to sort out. Meanwhile, Jimmis advice is ignored by Vivien at a car auction, and Eva suggests buying a house with him

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie feels Wills bedside manner needs improving and makes it clear she has no respect for his privileged upbringing. She orders him to go out for a drink with her and discovers a few surprising secrets about his background. Diane returns to work for the first time since her sisters death, but some of her colleagues think she hasnt given herself enough time to deal with the grief, or taking on the responsibility of raising Joannas child. When she breaks down during the shift, it becomes clear she blames herself for the tragedy

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The arrival of an administrative adviser leads Jay to start a petition against her bureaucratic style, throwing the ED into mutiny, but his actions have unintended consequences. Ruth tries to persuade Edward they should come clean about their sham marriage, while Warren becomes aware of the tension between Adam and Kirsty. Guest starring Fiona Allen (Waterloo Road)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A figure from the past has come back to haunt Rawton with sinister packages and mystery calls. Lennox steps in to offer his support, but has his work cut out squaring up to the dangerous psychopath

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As events in the Vic reach their shocking climax, Whitney realises that Billie Jackson is in way over his head. Meanwhile, Jack and Chelsea get spicy in the Argee Bhajee

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney gives Billie an ultimatum, while Carol fears for her sons future. Janine and Ryans new money-making scheme almost shows their soft side

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Calum cooks a meal, but it takes so long to prepare his guests drink too much and pass out before it is ready. Meanwhile, James encounters a dog that faints to gain attention. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn. First broadcast in 1988

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: Ken and Orrin Plot against Charles while he is busy voicing his concerns over Laura and Geralds relationship

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to look into the drowning of a former Miss Stratford-upon-Avon. The duo begin by uncovering the beauty queens rather dark past. Jamie Glover and Sasha Behar guest star, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon gets a passion for democracy - and decides to buy Darryls vote from Tracey for the local by-election. Robert Llewellyn guests stars, with Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo discovers that an infirm friend needs help to bury his pet in the churchyard, and calls on Alvin and Entwistle to carry out the deed. However, the mission becomes complicated when the police begin to suspect the trio are up to no good. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Burt Kwouk and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Behind Every Bush There Is Not Necessarily a Howard
programme.desc: Hobbo enlists his friends in a scheme to help Toby win back his ex-wife - but finds she only has eyes for Morton. Meanwhile, Howard tries to keep Pearl from interfering with his bike ride with Marina, and Cooper and Walsh test a new piece of in-car technology. Long-running rural comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Trevor Bannister, Peter Sallis and Burt Kwouk

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Pearl throws Howard out of the house, prompting him to declare he has sworn off women for good - but Hobbo and friends take it upon themselves to get him to talk to Marina first. Meanwhile, Cooper and Walsh have their car valeted after receiving complaints from the sergeant, Glenda tries to whip herself and Barry into shape in time for Glorias wedding, and Hobbo finds it difficult to avoid Miss Davenports attentions at the library

programme.title: Queens of Mystery
programme.desc: Part two of two. Contending with another murder and a missing lead actress, the theatre company is forced to change the cast around, but the production is a mess. Despite Matties objections, Beth and Cat continue to pursue leads on their own

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the groups past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandras private and professional worlds become intertwined when her mother has a fall that means she needs to go into a care home. Her first choice is revealed to be the scene of a suspicious death a year earlier, leading Jack, Gerry and Brian to go undercover at the home. Drama, guest starring June Whitfield, Sheila Hancock and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The Lynleys are looking forward to becoming parents, but Helen succumbs to temptation and dabbles in her husbands latest case. However, her past involvement with the victim unwittingly puts her in the firing line, with devastating consequences for everyone. Jemma Redgrave and Barbara Flynn guest star, with Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Lesley Vickerage

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Calum cooks a meal, but it takes so long to prepare his guests drink too much and pass out before it is ready. Meanwhile, James encounters a dog that faints to gain attention. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn. First broadcast in 1988

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part Two
programme.desc: As Judith and Suzie work with the police to find the killer, tensions rise among Becks friends in the house. Guest starring Harry Enfield

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Chris inherits a Greek cafe, which Sharon runs on his behalf - with Tracey as her skivvy and Dorien making the sandwiches. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CID officers Lennox, Proctor and Stanton spend the night in a building reputed to be haunted as part of a covert customs operation. At first they dismiss the idea of ghosts as pure fantasy and keep each other amused by telling horror stories - but then a suspect they are monitoring suddenly disappears into thin air

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Following the death of a gang leader on a nearby estate, Eva clashes with a local charismatic preacher who feels the solution to street crime is prayer patrols in the area. Meanwhile, Vivien asks Julia to give her a driving lesson. Cathy Tyson guest stars, with Angela Lonsdale and Anita Carey

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Melody helps a woman come to terms with her daughters death, after the doctor is hospitalised with gallstones. Julia and Vivien have a bad driving lesson, and Ruth is made to sit outside in the cold all night by Davey after lying to Michelle about why she missed a meeting at the Campus surgery

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie fails in her latest attempt to seduce Will, but has much more success with Mubbs. Lisa is furious to find Mickie, Donna and Jess having a party at their flat, while a patients wife is horrified to discover her husband is having an affair. Terri Dwyer guest stars

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor hires a daily to help out around the house, but comes to regret his decision, and a visit to the fish shop unleashes all manner of horrors. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson

programme.desc: A power cut on a sweltering summer night gives Victor and Margaret time to reflect on their lives together and look forward, without enthusiasm, to a visit from their elderly neighbours. Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Roy Hudd star

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: To Daphnes delight Sam packs in his hobby of taxidermy, but takes up another - their new next-door neighbour Rhonda. Comedy, starring Angela Thorne, Michael Elphick, Lysette Anthony and Ray Burdis

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: Daphne confronts Sams new girlfriend, Angie gets a screen test for a TV commercial, and Nick spies on Sam and Daphne with his telephoto lens. Comedy, starring Angela Thorne

programme.desc: Daphne starts to warm to Sam, prompting him to break up with Rhonda - who does not take it well. Nick offers his article to a tabloid editor. Angela Thorne and Michael Elphick star

programme.desc: First episode of the comedy about the romance between a couple with differing cultures and backgrounds. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star alongside Rob Brydon, Alison Steadman, Ruth Jones and James Corden

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Dave is unhappy about Nessas plans to let Smithy see the baby, while Stacey ensures Gavin sticks to his promise as she tries to find a new job. Meanwhile, Dawn and Pete join Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night. Comedy, starring Joanna Page and Mathew Horne

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony returns from holiday sporting an ill-advised beard, which he mistakenly thinks is bound to impress Deborah. Gary, meanwhile, has a problem of a different kind - Dorothy has moved in and he is not so sure it is a good idea after all, especially once she starts filling the flat with girly things. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin

programme.desc: Ted gets up to his usual tricks, using every money-raising fiddle in the book to fend off bailiffs threatening him with prison for failing to pay maintenance to his ex-wife. Paul Shane and Simon Cadell star in Jimmy Perry and David Crofts holiday camp comedy

programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

Tony makes an unwise purchase when he buys a snake from a man in the pub

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffreys wife sends her new boyfriend to the camp to ask him for a divorce. When the professor is unwilling to co-operate, the pair begin digging for evidence. Classic comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin the Teenager falls ill and Tim Nice-But-Dim meets a kindred spirit in Dick Nice-But-Thick. Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they cant resist getting involved with Thomass tale as well

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie puts Marlene under pressure to fit in with the locals, so she decides to take up cookery - an idea that does not go down well with her family. She orders her husband to pick blackberries for a pie, causing him to inadvertently trespass on the land of his anti-English neighbour. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: A Close Shave

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Dave is unhappy about Nessas plans to let Smithy see the baby, while Stacey ensures Gavin sticks to his promise as she tries to find a new job. Meanwhile, Dawn and Pete join Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night. Comedy, starring Joanna Page and Mathew Horne

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony returns from holiday sporting an ill-advised beard, which he mistakenly thinks is bound to impress Deborah. Gary, meanwhile, has a problem of a different kind - Dorothy has moved in and he is not so sure it is a good idea after all, especially once she starts filling the flat with girly things. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted gets up to his usual tricks, using every money-raising fiddle in the book to fend off bailiffs threatening him with prison for failing to pay maintenance to his ex-wife. Paul Shane and Simon Cadell star in Jimmy Perry and David Crofts holiday camp comedy

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Dont Wait Up
programme.desc: Circumstances remind Tom of the differences between the NHS and private practice, while Toby faces the surprising repercussions of a change in his life

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a cereal killer is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell, but Gromit is more concerned that his master might be the killers next victim

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A glamorous perfume saleswoman arrives on the shop floor, prompting a disgruntled Mrs Slocombe to plot the newcomers downfall. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility in New York State to meet Sheila Davalloo, who has been convicted for murdering Anna Lisa Raymundo. However, in a bizarre twist to the story, Sheila has not yet started her 50-year sentence. First she must finish the 25 years she was given for the attempted murder of her husband. Sheila has remained silent about the killing for 14 years, so Piers tries to get her to finally open up, while also looking into why she wanted both of her victims out of the way

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Transgender inmate Franklin falls in love with a fellow prisoner, but they must keep their relationship a secret or face being placed in solitary confinement. A fight breaks out over a carton of milk, and a pregnant prisoner trying to recover from heroin addiction faces losing a place on a treatment programme - which could result in her baby being born in prison and taken from her

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary Americas Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Gemma and Gorka: Life Behind the Lens
programme.desc: Gemma gets a postpartum check-up and starts a new fitness regime, while Mia and Gorka share a daddy-daughter Valentines date

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: After supporting an elderly relative through a tough time, Carolyn, Brad and their family are searching for a home of their own

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of Manchester couple Rebecca and Steve, whose life was changed for ever when they found out they were expecting triplets. Two years on, Rebecca is becoming depressed by the state of their small, damp, dilapidated house, which is unsafe for the children. The builders have just nine days to bring the property up to date and give this tired mum and dad the space they need for their active offspring. Luckily, local tradespeople and other members of the community turn out in force to help

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: Stacey joins former glamour model Carla Bellucci for her 40th birthday weekend. Carla has built her career around creating noisy and controversial headlines about herself and her family, and Stacey discovers why she has chosen a lifestyle which has earned her the title of Britains most hated woman

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Heroin addict Missy is regularly visited by a sugar daddy, who gives her the cash she needs to fund her habit. But as she awaits trial for kidnapping, she has to decide whether she is willing to give up a life of drugs and money in order to get her children back. Meanwhile, the romance between transgender prisoner Franklin and fellow inmate Leslie continues to blossom. Only able to meet in educational classes, will their relationship be discovered by the officers?

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Educated Julia does not match the typical image of a hardened criminal but has been charged with the murder of her ex-boyfriend. Now she must learn to navigate jail for the first time - all while proclaiming her innocence. Rachel feels the full weight of prison rule after failing a drugs test, while Missy, who is facing trial for kidnapping and home invasion, discovers she could face up to 20 years. How will the mum-of-three cope with such a long term?

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Under the Knife
programme.desc: The roving broadcaster travels to California, the birthplace of plastic surgery, to investigate the obsession with beauty and self-image. In his quest he meets Eve, a domineering stylist and her client Laura, and DJ Kris, who is resorting to liposuction to achieve the six-pack he so desires, leading Louis to consider having a nip and tuck himself

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The West Midlands Ambulance Service treat a man who stabbed a police dog, a woman with a suspected stroke and a man on his fourth bout of pneumonia

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Gemma and Gorka: Life Behind the Lens
programme.desc: A half-term holiday to Madrid gives the family a break, a chance to soak up some Spanish culture, and a long-awaited first meeting between Thiago and his grandparents

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit the Cotswolds and Somerset, where finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christophers needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Warrington, Cheshire, to meet electrician Dave McCartney, a healthy man until he had a stroke at work that has left him partially paralysed. Now he is forced to sleep in a hospital bed in his kitchen - but it wont be for much longer, as his old workmates team up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to adapt his home to his needs, with designer Charlie Luxton on hand to keep the boys in check

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Julie and Ryan moved back to Nashville to be closer to family, but are having trouble finding the perfect home

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: A musical couple is eager to find their forever home near Nashvilles legendary Music Row

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christophers needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a puppy who needs urgent treatment for a badly broken leg and a young Doberman who has spent most of her life in quarantine kennels

programme.title: Louis Theroux - The Most Hated Family in America
programme.desc: The journalist heads for Topeka, Kansas, to meet members of the controversial Westboro Baptist Church. Comprised almost entirely of members of the Phelps family, the organisation is vehement in its disapproval of homosexuality and followers have picketed the funerals of Iraq War soldiers in protest against Americas attitude toward gay people. Louis witnesses the emotional toll the clans strict rules have taken on its members, including 21-year-old Jael who says she will never have a boyfriend. He also meets Steve Drain, who set out to make a documentary about the group - and ended up as one of its number

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Americas Most Hated Family in Crisis
programme.desc: The reporter returns to Topeka, Kansas, to visit the Westboro Baptist Church, a notoriously homophobic organisation run by the Phelps family, whose activities he first documented in a 2007 film. On arrival, he discovers the members of the Church now regard him as a hate figure after watching his original documentary - and finds that increased scrutiny from the outside world has led to their practices becoming increasingly bizarre. He also meets two women who left the Church, and learns how their decision led to them being ostracised by their parents

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest. They include a family of 12 in Colorados Rocky Mountains, who have isolated themselves for the past decade

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: When travelling musician and drug user Carmen goes into multiple seizures, officers call a Code Blue emergency as staff and paramedics scramble to respond

programme.desc: Crews help a young woman who fell down a flight of stairs and a shivering pensioner with possible sepsis, as well as a sparky 95-year-old whos had a fall

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Transgender inmate Franklin falls in love with a fellow prisoner, but they must keep their relationship a secret or face being placed in solitary confinement. programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary Americas Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo reaches Birmingham and discovers how a radical reformer made his mark on the city of a thousand trades. At Highbury Hall, he finds Joseph Chamberlains mansion home and hears how he established a political dynasty including a prime minister and a foreign secretary. Next stop is Cradley Heath, once one of five chain-making towns in the West Midlands. At the centre of Britains ceramic industry in Stoke-on-Trent, Michael visits Wedgwood, established by Queens Potter Josiah Wedgwood in 1759 and visited in 1913 by King George V and Queen Mary

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson is on the road to pick up a funky little Nissan Figaro and a sublime Ferrari 250 GT tribute car, and Paul Mathewson gets to try out his spanking new Scania T Cab

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasnt seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: As Hitlers troops advance, occupants in Ukraine, Belarus and Poland take the brunt of the occupying forces cruelty; and the first concentration camps are created

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: The documentary looks at how Britain identified Mikhail Gorbachev as a possible new contender for Soviet leader, believing he could be the ticket to better relations between East and West. Plus, how fragile steps towards diplomacy were threatened by the vested interests of other spies still stuck in the old world order

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts from Woodhorn Museum in Northumberland. Treasures brought in to be valued include a gold Rolex President wristwatch from the 1980s and a grotesque ceramic animal sculpture made by the eccentric Martin Brothers. Suzanne Zack is enchanted by a beautifully rendered pencil portrait by British artist Henry Ryland, while John Foster falls for a small Roman figure uncovered by a metal detectorist. Plus, Fiona learns tells the story of coal mining and the miners way of life in the north-east of England

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts visit Ham House in the London borough of Richmond, where items brought in to be valued include a necklace and bracelet found in a bag of compost, a chessboard artwork designed by film-maker Derek Jarman, and a rare Omega watch made to commemorate the Apollo-Soyuz space mission in 1975. Fiona Bruce meets members of the Punjabi community in Southall and looks at the work of the Poppy Factory

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

Plus, Fiona learns tells the story of coal mining and the miners way of life in the north-east of England

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across the Kalahari desert in southern Africa, one of the continents last great unspoilt wildernesses. He begins his journey in a small village in Namibias Nyae Nyae conservancy, where he sets out on a hunt for wildebeest with San trackers, who still live in the traditional way. After weeks of dusty travel, Simon ends his journey in the Okavango Delta, where seasonal rains turn the desert in to a vast oasis of greenery

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A cherished wartime iron workhorse needs to be hauled 160 miles from the Churnet Valley Railway in Staffordshire to a winter extravaganza in Oxfordshire

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. In the first episode, David takes a look at wheeled vehicles from the Penny Farthing bicycle to Stephensons rocket - as well as being reunited with an original Reliant Regal Supervan, as used in Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek encourages the team to buy a Francis Barnett Plover at auction. Plus, the wheels come off the restoration of an MG Metro

programme.desc: Derek thinks a 29,000-mile Ford Capri should be kept original. Plus, a Hercules womens racing bike from the 50s

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.sub-title: Air Raid Shelter and Pedal-Powered Washing Machine
programme.desc: The team reinvent a vintage DIY air raid shelter as a hi-tech garden shed. Plus, Jenny and Ellis work on a pedal-powered washing machine.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey across Northern Ireland to Argyll and Bute. Beginning in Newry, he finds a specially chartered train that would deliver demonstrators campaigning for Irish Home Rule to a rally in the town. On the border between Northern Ireland and the Republic at Glaslough, Michael discovers a christening robe belonging to Sir Winston Churchill

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Belfast to Portrush, he investigates the work of author and Narnia creator CS Lewis and learns how religion influenced Lewiss work. Michael also visits the Royal Portrush Golf Club to find out how Irelands female population dominated the fairways at the time of his guide

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Northern Ireland across the water to Scotland. Leaving from the seaport of Larne, he reaches Stranraer and the Mull of Galloway, where in a lighthouse built by railway engineer Robert Stevenson he discovers a magnificent machine, installed at the turn of the 20th-century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Glasgow to Cumbrae, with his early 20th-century Bradshaws guide in hand, he is put to work behind the scenes at Glasgows circular subway. He also explores the future of shipbuilding on the Clyde and hears how one woman led a successful mass protest against high rents in the citys notorious tenements

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: In the 100th episode, Derek heads to Northumberland to collect a rare 1975 NSU RO 80, Jack deals with a Mini Mayfair stuck in a barn, and an Aston Martin DBS husk is auctioned

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: On the way to pick up two lovely 70s Lotus sports cars, Derek takes to the skies for his first ever helicopter flight in a 1967 Bell 47 chopper

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: The Red Army develops strength and Hitlers Spring Offensive is stopped at Stalingrad; and in the largest ghetto the inhabitants fight back in the Warsaw Uprising

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: In 1985, a very tense time, a young Mikhail Gorbachev is instated as the new leader of the Soviet Union. East and West have been publicly stockpiling nuclear weapons for decades, neither side wanting to appear weak. Plus, the spying game threatens the first tentative moves towards peace

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Bishops Palace in Wells, Somerset. Items brought in to be valued include a Delft plaque from a Bristol pub, decorated with a drinking song by composer Purcell, which could be of national importance, a portrait of an unknown First World War army officer, and a collection of movie memorabilia, including rare posters. Plus, Basic, Better and Best features a range of items by Finnish designers

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh. Finds include a set of traditional Highland dress that once belonged to a clan chieftain, a selection of medals awarded to a pioneering diver, a silver trophy in the shape of a rams head, and a 19th-century bust by sculptor Thomas Stuart Burnett. Fiona gets the chance to take a closer look at a plant specimen collected by Charles Darwin during his voyage on HMS Beagle, and is challenged to put a selection of ceramic piglets by the Wemyss firm in order of value

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a solid-looking 1969 MGB for the team to look out for at auction. After winning it is sent to a father-and-son team to restore

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Dave alerts the team to a 1979 Lotus Esprit S3. Its an expensive buy and a serious risk, but its also a thoroughbred classic in need of a proper recommission

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: The Red Army develops strength and Hitlers Spring Offensive is stopped at Stalingrad; and in the largest ghetto the inhabitants fight back in the Warsaw Uprising

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: In 1985, a very tense time, a young Mikhail Gorbachev is instated as the new leader of the Soviet Union. Plus, Basic, Better and Best features a range of items by Finnish designers

programme.title: Operation Arctic
programme.desc: Originally broadcast live from the Arctic, this series follows the struggles of polar bears living on this frozen tundra and highlights the impact that environmental changes are having on the bears and the changing world around them. Simon Reeve, Kate Humble and Gordon Buchanan examine the effects of climate change and the struggles these bears now face as the sea ice continues to melt at an alarming rate

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Hunting Outback Gold
programme.desc: In the search for billions of dollars in gold, historian Tanya uncovers a shocking new theory that flips the hunt to the east

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a solid-looking 1969 MGB for the team to look out for at auction. After winning it is sent to a father-and-son team to restore

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Dave alerts the team to a 1979 Lotus Esprit S3. Its an expensive buy and a serious risk, but its also a thoroughbred classic in need of a proper recommission

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil finds a pinball machine that should be simple to repair, but just when Rob thinks hes got the flippers flipping, another tricky obstacle gets in the way

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil relives his childhood memories with a classic Scalextric, but its a new challenge for Rob who never owned one as a child and has never fixed one either

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michaels final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex. At Cowley, he marvels at an automated state-of-the-art production line that produces 1,000 Minis a day. Michael probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford and is delighted to find that cars for export are loaded in Italian Job style on to trains at the factorys own railhead

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers and in Henley-on-Thames he slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance for Michael to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore Londons new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a 1970s beige with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michaels final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex. At Cowley, he marvels at an automated state-of-the-art production line that produces 1,000 Minis a day. Michael probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford and is delighted to find that cars for export are loaded in Italian Job style on to trains at the factorys own railhead

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers and in Henley-on-Thames he slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance for Michael to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore Londons new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a 1970s beige with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: In Kursk, a battlefield the size of Wales, the tide turns, and the Soviets push back, but as Germany retreats, they destroy all evidence of their genocidal activities

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The fossil record shows that the arapaima fish has roamed the waters of the Amazon River since the Jurassic age, and uses its bony tongue to crush prey against the roof of its mouth before swallowing. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade encountered the species once before and came out second best, so this time he is taking no chances as he investigates every aspect of this huge creature

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: After finding out about the hospitals staffing problems, Jill offers to cut short her maternity leave and return to work, while Matron notices that Catherine is doing her best to avoid Adam. Out in Africa, Jeff faces the horrors of war and makes a promise to a dying woman - but faces difficulties in keeping his word

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the Derry legend, who discusses his colourful football career and the place GAA has in society

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, drama student Josiah, factory worker Daniel and support worker Tomas hope to win a date with make-up artist Chanel. All they have to do is find her the perfect outfit - limited only by time, budget and their own sense of style

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Flight attendant Chloe, law graduate Ivana and trampolining champion Suzie all hope to win a date with businessman Myles. To impress him, they must dress him, as he has no other information to go on than the outfits they choose

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the celebrity contestants are paired with a professional chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. Tonight, the remaining celebrities take on French cuisine as they battle to stay in the competition. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Clare native Vincent is attending the animal hospital with Porsche, his once speedy greyhound that he fears is on her last legs. Meanwhile, making the trip from Wicklow is Kealan Doyle and his pet Kunekune pigs Hank and Joey, who are lined up for a badly needed operation. Sapphire, a Shibu Inu dog is brought to the Vet Hospital after a sudden decline in her health, but her medical needs come at a huge financial cost for owner Bernadette

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as Abbey Clancy, Pasha Kovalev and Katherine Ryan cook their best date night dishes for their partners Peter Crouch, Rachel Riley and Bobby Kootstra in the first three-way cooking battle of the series. The winner will secure a place in the semi-final while the bottom two will fight for their place in the competition in the cook-off

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Tracey from Middlesex, Geoff from Southport, Duran from Sunderland and Nick from Belfast pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Darren, Kerry, Mustafa and Gugu vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin returns from Africa with Biafran orphan Nkeshi, and shocks his colleagues when they realise he has smuggled the child into the country illegally. The presence of an unhappy celebrity causes a paparazzi furore, and nearly costs Frankie his job. Dr Weatherill deals with an uncooperative alcoholic on her first day back at work. Medical drama, starring Paul Fox

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: The show returns to London, where roofer Bruce, Essex boy Lewis and physiotherapist Mirkan all hope to impress marketing manager Leila with their style choices. Whoever dresses her the best will get to join her for a romantic first date, but which lucky fella will it be?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Michael Jackson impersonator Mitch, bar manager Aiden and student JJ try to impress Becky, a student from Leeds, with their outfit choices

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Labrador pup Bonnie must undergo life-saving heart surgery, while ragdoll cat owner John needs to uncover what is wrong with his favourite feline

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans its an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cooking contest as Carol Vorderman, Linford Christie and Ellie Simmonds try to impress their loved ones with family favourite dishes they serve up in the studio. With double the amount of meals to deliver this time, the pressure is on as they battle it out for a place in the semi-final, while the stars who fail to impress the shows chefs head into the dreaded cook-off

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Rosie from Chelmsford, Steve originally from Wakefield, Kallan from Cardiff and Seb from Wareham take on one of the countrys finest quiz brains

programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.desc: A renegade general and his crack team of mercenaries take over the abandoned fortress prison of Alcatraz and threaten San Francisco with toxic nerve-gas rockets to extort a massive ransom. programme.title: Friends
